# UC-17.1.50 · Cisco ISE — MDM/UEM Connector Health and Compliance Drift

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*If a phone or laptop is enrolled in the company's mobile-management system and that system says 'this device is not OK', ISE should block it. We watch the conversation between ISE and the management system — and ring the bell when that conversation breaks.*

---

## Description

Detects MDM/UEM connector failures, auth issues, and rate-limit hits on ISE-to-MDM API integrations. Without MDM connector health, ISE authorization decisions that depend on `mdm_compliance` or `mdm_jailbroken` revert to non-compliance default (typically more permissive).

## Value

BYOD environments rely on MDM compliance to gate corporate-resource access. A broken Intune connector means non-compliant devices receive the same access as compliant ones. Detecting integration failures within 10 minutes preserves compliance enforcement.

## Implementation

Confirm *MDM Server* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller. Schedule every 10 min. Throttle suppression on `mdm_vendor` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Revoke ISE's API token in the MDM admin console. ISE emits 60142 on next API call. UC-17.1.50 fires.

### Negative scenario

Restore the token. Connection re-establishes. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- MDM connector configured.
- `mdm_vendors.csv` lookup with `vendor`, `is_critical`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *MDM Server* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 10 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — MDM Connector Health":
- Per-vendor status.
- Compliance distribution.
- Top-N rate-limit hits.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on connector failure.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Auth failures recur** — Token rotation needed.
- **Rate limits sustained** — Tune ISE's MDM polling cadence.
- **`mdm_compliance` always 'unknown'** — MDM provider doesn't expose the field; check provider model.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60140, 60141, 60142, 60143, 60144))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:mdm_status")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event count(eval(MessageCode=="60141")) as drops count(eval(MessageCode=="60142")) as auth_failures count(eval(MessageCode=="60143")) as rate_limits by mdm_vendor
| where drops > 1 OR auth_failures > 0 OR rate_limits > 5
```

## Visualization

(1) Per-vendor connector status. (2) Compliance distribution panel. (3) Top-N: rate-limit hits by vendor.

## Known False Positives

- **MDM vendor maintenance** — vendor-side maintenance windows.
- **Token refresh window** — Some MDM APIs require token refresh; brief auth_failures during the refresh.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — MDM Integration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_compliance_segmentation.html)
